description: Verify print readiness of Adobe Illustrator file: color mode, bleed/trim marks, font handling, and resolution.
prompt_instructions: Scan the document text extracted from an Adobe Illustrator (.ai) file.
Check if the document uses CMYK color mode (look for CMYK references or color space declarations).
Look for references to bleed area, trim marks, or crop marks.
Determine if fonts are outlined (converted to paths) or embedded in the document.
Check for resolution references and whether they meet the 300 DPI minimum for print.

Format the output as JSON: { "color_mode": "<CMYK/RGB/Mixed/Unknown>", "is_cmyk": <true/false>, "bleed_marks_present": <true/false>, "trim_marks_present": <true/false>, "fonts_outlined_or_embedded": <true/false>, "resolution_dpi": <number or null>, "resolution_adequate": <true/false/null>, "status": "<PrintReady/NotPrintReady/Inconclusive>" }.
Set status to 'PrintReady' only if color mode is CMYK, fonts are outlined or embedded, and resolution is 300+ DPI.
Set status to 'Inconclusive' if key information cannot be determined from the extracted text.
Respond ONLY with the JSON output.
